content: Contact tracing protocol: (1) Interview cases within 24-48 hours of diagnosis about contacts during infectious period; (2) Define close contact (typically >15 minutes within 6 feet over 24 hours); (3) Notify contacts within 24 hours, provide exposure information without revealing case identity; (4) Recommend quarantine duration based on incubation period (e.g., 14 days for many diseases); (5) Monitor contacts daily for symptoms; (6) Test symptomatic contacts immediately. Digital tools supplement manual tracing but have privacy concerns. Resource requirements: approximately 1 tracer per 30 cases for effective tracing.
source: CDC contact tracing guidance, WHO field epidemiology manual
